title: Amauropelta negligens
intro: Amauropelta negligens, also known as the Negligent Maidenhair, is a type of fern native to South America, characterized by its delicate, lacy fronds. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Amauropelta negligens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often in combination with other medicinal plants to enhance its therapeutic effects.
